task_completion.description: Agent logged into the NOSH ChartingSystem.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NOSH ChartingSystem dashboard or interface is visible in a web browser.

task_completion.description: Agent accessed the correct patient's chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient chart is open, and the patient's name or PID matches the contents of the target files.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the patient's active problem list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Problem List' or 'Issues' section of the patient's chart is open, or an 'Add Problem' dialog is visible.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct diagnosis and ICD-10 code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The diagnosis field contains 'Essential Hypertension' or 'Essential (primary) hypertension' and the ICD-10 code 'I10'.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct onset date and status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The onset date field is set to '2024-08-15' and the status dropdown or toggle is set to 'Active'.

task_completion.description: The diagnosis is saved and persists in the patient's record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The problem list displays the saved entry for 'Essential Hypertension' (I10) with an 'Active' status and onset date of '2024-08-15' without any unsaved changes indicators.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the NOSH web interface rather than using backend shortcuts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the NOSH GUI, not just terminal windows or API testing tools.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database commands to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing MySQL or SQLite 'INSERT' or 'UPDATE' commands related to the problem list.

integrity.description: Agent read the target patient files to identify the patient.
integrity.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing commands like 'cat /tmp/target_patient.txt' or a text editor displaying the file contents.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target patient's name and ID must be retrieved from /tmp/target_patient.txt and /tmp/target_patient_pid.txt. The official ICD-10-CM terminology for I10 is 'Essential (primary) hypertension', which is acceptable and correct for 'Essential Hypertension'. The required onset date is exactly 2024-08-15.
